title: Homology and cell structure of nilpotent spaces
journal: Transactions of the American Mathematical Society
abstract:
Let 

  
    A
    A
  

 and 

  
    X
    X
  

 denote finitely dominated nilpotent 

  
    
      CW
    
    {\text {CW}}
  

 complexes. We are interested in questions relating the homology groups of such spaces to their cell structure and homotopy type. We solve a problem posed by Brown and Kahn, that of constructing nilpotent complexes of minimal dimension. When the fundamental group is finite, the three-dimensional complex we construct may not be finite; we then construct a finite six-dimensional complex. We investigate the set of possible cofibers of maps 

  
    
      A
      →
      X
    
    A \to X
  

, and find a severe restriction. When it is met and the fundamental group is finite, 

  
    X
    X
  

 can be constructed from 

  
    A
    A
  

 by attaching cells in a natural way. The restriction implies that the classical notion of homology decomposition has no application to nilpotent complexes. We show that the Euler characteristic of 

  
    X
    X
  

 must be zero. Several corollaries are derived to the theory of finitely dominated nilpotent complexes. Several of these results depend upon a purely algebraic theorem that we prove concerning the vanishing of homology of nilpotent modules over nilpotent groups.
